title: Open Systems: A Technology Status Report
author: Peter S. Kastner, John Logan, Thomas Willmott
abstract: This Aberdeen Group workbook provides a comprehensive status report on Open Systems as of January 1993, covering the transition from proprietary to open, distributed computing architectures. It assesses five key technology components — hardware microprocessors, systems software, application development toolsets, networking, and emerging architectures — and profiles major vendor positions and standards organizations. The study culminates in an ACTION framework for IS executives managing the organizational and economic challenges of open systems adoption.
importance_rationale: One of the earliest structured frameworks for enterprise open systems planning, synthesizing microprocessor, OS, networking, and standards trends into actionable guidance for IS executives during a pivotal industry transition.
relevance_rationale: The architectural principles of three-tier distributed computing and component-based infrastructure remain foundational; specific vendor and product details are historical.
prescience_rationale: Predictions proved accurate: Intel x86 dominance grew as forecast, Windows/DOS dominated the near-term desktop, client-server displaced proprietary midrange, and integration responsibility shifted to customers as described.
